description: Tracks User Timing API marks for frontend performance, providing a more accurate view of perceived performance than standard Web Vitals metrics like LCP, which can be affected by layout changes and heuristics.

options.content:
Resources 📊 Sitespeed Results 📁 Measurement Project 📝 Share Feedback
Dashboards 🗺️ User Journey 📈 Page Summary

panels.description: All User Timing marks created on the page using the [User Timing API](https://www.w3.org/TR/user-timing/).
panels.title: User Timings Marks  [$function]

panels.description: All User Timing marks created on the page using the [User Timing API](https://www.w3.org/TR/user-timing/).
panels.title: User Timings Measures [$function]

panels.description: The total number of iframes used on the page.
The $function cache expire time of all responses for the page.

targets.target: alias($base.$path.$testname.pageSummary.$group.$page.$browser.$connectivity.pagexray.expireStats.$function, 'The $function cache expire time of all responses')

panels.description: The $function last modified time of all responses for the page.

targets.target: alias($base.$path.$testname.pageSummary.$group.$page.$browser.$connectivity.pagexray.lastModifiedStats.$function, 'The $function last modified time of all responses')

panels.description: The average DOM depth of the page.
The max depth of DOM elements on the page.
The total number of iframes used on the page.
The CPU metrics are collected from Chrome when you add --chrome.timeline to your run. The trace log is analyzed and sorted by type.
panels.title: CPU time spent per event

panels.description: Show number of errors from Browsertime and other tools.
panels.title: Errors collecting the metrics

title: fe-perf: Sitespeed - User Timings
